title: Dasiphora glabra
intro: Dasiphora glabra, also known as shrubby cinquefoil, is a flowering plant species native to mountainous regions of Asia, characterized by its shrub-like growth habit and yellow or pink flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Dasiphora glabra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dasiphora glabra is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Dasiphora glabra is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a member of the tribe Rosoideae and is closely related to other rosaceous plants such as roses and apples. The genus Dasiphora is often considered to be a single species, with Dasiphora glabra being the most widely accepted species within this genus. However, some sources may group Dasiphora glabra as a synonym for Dasiphora fruticosa, which is a shrub native to China and the Himalayan region. Further research may be necessary to determine the most accurate classification of Dasiphora glabra.
uses: Dasiphora glabra has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have analgesic and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating various ailments. In traditional Chinese medicine, Dasiphora glabra is believed to help with digestion and relieve symptoms of fever. Its roots and leaves are also used to create teas and infusions, which are thought to aid in respiratory issues and reduce stress. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses, but Dasiphora glabra has shown promise as a medicinal plant in various studies.

properties: Dasiphora glabra, also known as Arctic blumea or Arctic broom, is a shrub native to the mountainous regions of Asia. constituents: Dasiphora glabra contains flavonoids and triterpenoid saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Additionally, the plant is rich in phenolic acids, including ferulic and sinapic acids. The triterpenoid saponins present in Dasiphora glabra may also contribute to its immunomodulatory effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents and their potential applications.
